title: Interstellar: Galaxy
body:
# Interstellar
Welcome to Interstellar, a mod that allows you to traverse the universe! Well, just the Moon, rocky planets, asteroid belt, and Galilean Moons right now, but there are plans!
## Luna
Luna, more commonly known as the Moon, can be traveled to in these sort of realm crossover biomes, the Lunized Wastes, where moon dust is dropped from the Moon and piles up on the ground. 
### The Portal
You make a lunar portal like a nether portal, except you use compacted moon dust instead of obsidian, and Lunite instead of Flint and Steel.
### Suffocation
Watch out! Setting foot on Luna can be deadly without a lunite space suit! Lunite can be found underground in the Lunized Wastes or under Luna itself. Once you have crafted the armor, you are free to traverse the low-grav environment of Luna!
### Temperature
Of course, since the Moon has no atmosphere, it has extreme temperature swings! To protect yourself from burning to a crisp or becoming a human icicle, you'll need TempKote®! Slap that stuff on your helmet and you'll have a fully conditioned space-suit! Now you're ready for anything Luna throws at you!

## Mars
1.1.1 is here and Mars is ready to be explored! If you want to take on this new challenge, read well!
### The Portal
Unlike Luna, Mars doesn't have any sort of crossover biome. So how do you get there? It's simpler than it seems. You'll need about 91 iron ingots. Craft all but 1 into blocks and smelt them to oxidize them. Once that's done, make the portal frame. To ignite the portal, smelt the remaining iron ingot twice to make Iron Oxide Dust, and sprinkle that onto the portal to light it up!
### Suffocation
Much like Luna, Mars has very little oxygen. However, it does have a bit, which means you have about 2.4x longer before you meet your fate in space. But, nonetheless, you still need a Lunite spacesuit.
### Temperature
Mars's temperature swings aren't too different from Luna's, except colder. Get that TempKote®, and good luck!

## Venus
1.2.1 has dropped! Venus is now available for exploration! This one's a little harsher than the rest, so beware!
### The Portal
Much like Mars, Venus's portal is made from a vanilla block: basalt. Yes, I know, you have to go to the Nether... again. But, it is well worth it. You also need Scorched Flint and Steel to ignite the portal (obtained by smelting Flint and Steel)
### Suffocation
Unlike the others, you only have a brief 6.25 seconds before perishing on Venus due to its thick, crushing carbon dioxide atmosphere, so good luck exploring Venus without a suit!
### Temperature
Venus is the hottest planet yet, at 464°C (867°F for those who can't read metric)! So, you'll definitely need a TempKoted® spacesuit! It's so hot that water easily boils, so good luck cooling off (you're smart, you'll find a way)
### Navigation
Venus's thick atmosphere creates heavy "fog", so it's easy to get lost or turned around. Make sure you always know where you're going! Leave a block trail or something, idk.
### Resources
Remember when I said it was all worth it? On Venus, a gem called Venite is very abundant. It is around 4.1x as powerful as netherite and 3.17x as fast as gold. It can be used for tools and a spacesuit. It is a natural insulator as well, so you don't need that TempKote® anymore!
## Mercury
1.3 has arrived, and Mercury beckons for exploration! This is also the fourth and final rocky planet in this mod, so I can now move on to other areas of the solar system!
### The Portal
To build this portal, you need to go to Venus to collect the required item. Specifically, a yellow dust known as sulfur. You'll need 91 to make the portal, with 90 being used for blocks and 1 to light the portal.
### Suffocation
Similar to Luna, you have 12.5 seconds, so grab Lunite or Venite armor before trying to tackle this planet.
### Temperature
Similar to Venus, but slightly cooler.
### Navigation
The surface of Mercury is unstable, so make sure of what's under that silicate dust before trying to complete your Interstellar collection again.
### Resources
The third full gear set has dropped! Hermite is even more powerful than Venite, and it's also a natural insulator as well (definitely not because I'm just lazy and don't want to double the amount of helmets in the mod), so you can traverse freely around the planets knowing that you're safe.
## The Asteroid Belt
1.4 is here! The asteroid belt now marks the border between the inner and outer planets!
### The Portal
This portal is the easiest so far - you only need to find a mountain and a fire charge and make a stone portal!
### Suffocation
Like may places in this mod, you only have 12.5 seconds before you suffocate. So, y'know... grab a space-suit!
### Temperature
Cold, dark, desolate. You might need Temp-Kote or insulative armor here.
### Navigation
The Asteroid belt is much like the end, but it has a new feature - variable gravity! So, once you get over the void, there's nothing below you to pull you in, so you just float! I sure hope nobody uses this as an exploit! By default, the game checks for your gravity every 11 ticks (sorry OCD people), but if you want it to be quicker, there's a gamerule to change it. Here are some default values:
- 0: every tick
- 4: 4 times per second
- 9: twice per second
- 10: default
- 19: once every second
- 99: every five seconds - I hope your comupter isn't so much of a potato that it needs this!
### Resources
Four gear sets are now in the mod! This time, it's meteorite gear! It is nearly identical to Hermite, except one key difference: it isn't a good material for making space-suits! So, don't try to leave Earth while wearing meteorite armor! It will end with all of your stuff being lost on a foreign celestial body, I can almost guarantee it.
## Galileo Region
Jupiter's Galilean Moons! Only Ganymede right now, but look forward to more Galilean moons in the near future!
### The Portals
Dirty Ice† frame with corresponding dust‡ to ignite.

† Galilean Dust†† + Ice -> Dirty Ice

†† Crushed Ice††† + Sulfur Dust -> Galilean Dust

††† Ice -> Crushed Ice x9

‡ Galilean Dust†† + Brown Dye -> Ganymede Dust

### Suffocation
At this point, do I even have to tell you? 12.5 seconds until you dai, simple as that! So get a spacesuit.
### Temperature
Moderately cold. Might want Temp-Kote or insulative armor.
### Navigation
Solid Dirty Ice surface with Packed Ice subsurface and Ice as rock.
### Resources
Useless Galilean Dust. Woohoo.

## Recommended/Required Mods
There are many mods that tie this experience together. Astrocraft is required, because it looks really cursed without it! Sinytra Connector is also required to attach these mods together. However, Sinytra Connector is not compatible with the latest version this mod is on (ahem Sinytra...) so it's going to look pretty cursed until either I find out how to edit the skybox, or Sinytra needs to get their mods up to date. I have recently added a 1.21.1 version that actually does work with the other two, but apparently the observerPlanet option didn't exist in that version because it didn't even have a config, so I'm not going to continue that. And finally, Tough as Nails is highly recommended, to tie together the climate experience.

## What's Next?
This mod wouldn't be called Interstellar without plans for other planets! Here's the plans:
## 1.x.x: Our Solar System
### ✔️ 1.0.1: Luna
### ✔️ 1.1.x: Mars

### ✔️ 1.4.x: Asteroid Belt
### ~~1.5.x: Ceres (guys, Ceres is a dwarf planet, not an asteroid!)~~

## 2.x.x: The Kepler-1 System
### No plans for order yet

## Love this mod? Have a bug to report? Want something added?
Contact me on Discord (@EvBe19). I'll add you to the Interstellar server within a few hours, depending on the day. Make sure to @mention me. Or, y'know... click the link to join the discord server if it works. (below on phone, to the right anywhere else) If it doesn't, check back tomorrow.
## Want to directly support me and my team?
PATREON PATREON PATREON. Join for cool perks and exclusive insights on the mod!
## Want to watch cool Minecraft content!
Check out my YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@EvBe19), and maybe consider subscribing! It helps get my content out to more people and it's totally free - and you'll get weekly notifications for my new videos!
